name: Peggy Lee
abstract: Norma Deloris Egstrom (May 26, 1920 – January 21, 2002), known professionally as Peggy Lee, was an American jazz and popular music singer, songwriter, and actress whose career spanned seven decades. From her beginning as a vocalist on local radio to singing with Benny Goodman's big band, Lee created a sophisticated persona, writing music…

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions 'Peggy Lee, working with the Lou Levy orchestra' as a date title/context, but the interviewee (Hubert Laws) does not respond to or acknowledge this mention, nor does he mention Peggy Lee in his response.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Horn Shirley
about_quotes.evidence: I met her once and I was really overwhelmed, recent years, you know. She was at Antoine‘s. I went to see her, and she wasn‘t . . . she invited me to come to the hotel, so I did.
about_quotes.doc_title: Horn Shirley — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: J. C. Higginbotham
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript contains no mention of Peggy Lee by the interviewee, nor any interaction or relationship described between J.C. Higginbotham and Peggy Lee.

about_quotes.speaker: David Finck
about_quotes.evidence: I played for Peggy Lee... I remember playing with her and we played “Them There Eyes.”
about_quotes.doc_title: David Finck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 29, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: And that was my thing before going with Peggy Lee, with Jimmy Rowles, Joe Mondragon, Jack Costanzo was on bongos... And we stayed with him for about a year, year and a half, and played places — New York, we’d be on the road for six weeks, and we did the “Black Coffee” album in New York.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Candoli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Phoebe Jacobs and Jackie Harris
about_quotes.evidence: he knew that I worked with Peggy. And her daughter said that I knew Peggy better than she knew her own mother, because I traveled with her and lived with her from time to time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phoebe Jacobs and Jackie Harris interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 21, 2006
